title: entada zeylanica
intro: Entada zeylanica, also known as the Indian bead tree, is a perennial climber native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and southern India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. Entada zeylanica is often prepared as a herbal decoction or a paste, and it can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
